relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A new heaven and new earth appear; the old sea-bound order is gone.
New Jerusalem descends as adorned bride.
Divine dwelling is with humans.
Every tear is wiped; death, grief, crying, and pain pass away.
The throne says: I make all things new.
The thirsty receive water of life freely.
The conqueror inherits as child of Divine.
False and harmful patterns belong to second death.
The bride of the Lamb is shown.
The holy city descends from Divine glory.
Its radiance is crystal-bright.
Twelve gates carry Israel's tribal names.
The gates open to all directions.
Twelve foundations carry the apostles' names.
The city is measured with gold.
Its length, width, and height form holy fullness.
The wall is measured in human and messenger measure.
Jasper and transparent gold form the city.
Foundations shine with precious stones.
Every jewel adds to the city’s radiance.
Pearl gates and glass-gold street gleam.
There is no temple, for Divine and Lamb are temple.
The Lamb is the city’s lamp.
Nations walk by its light.
Its gates are never shut, and night is gone.
Nations bring glory and honor in.
Only truth and life enter the Lamb’s city.

contemplative_note: Revelation 21 is the homecoming of creation: city descends, tears end, nations enter light, and Divine presence becomes the temple.
